case_title: FRANK L. KING VS. SAN FRANCISCO EMPLOYEES' RETIREMENT SYSTEM
cause_of_action: WRITS OF MANDATE OR PROH., CERTI., ETC./ADMIN. AGEN

docket_entries.description: MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FILED BY PETITIONER KING, FRANK L.
ANSWER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION FILED BY RESPONDENT SAN FRANCISCO EMPLOYEES' RETIREMENT SYSTEM

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: PETITIONER FRANK KING MOTION TO AUGMENT RECORD IS DENIED. AS TO THE SEPTEMBER 16, 2012 SOCIAL SECURITY ADMINISTRATION DECISION, PETITIONER FAILED TO MAKE A THRESHOLD SHOWING THAT THE SOCIAL SECURITY ADMINISTRATION DECISION IS RELEVANT FOR THE PURPOSES OF ANALYZING PETITIONER'S APPLICATION FOR ORDINARY DISABILITY RETIREMENT, WHICH IS BASED ON A DIFFERENT LEGAL STANDARD. (SEE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 1094.5(E).) AS TO DR. ROSENBAUM'S MEDICAL REPORT, PETITIONER HAS FAILED TO SHOW THAT IT IS RELEVANT. DR. ROSENBAUM'S REPORT WAS BASED ON AN EXAM THAT TOOK PLACE IN APRIL 2012. PETITIONER HAS NOT SHOWN HOW THESE FINDINGS ARE RELEVANT TO THE ISSUE WHETHER PETITIONER WAS INCAPACITATED FOR THE PERFORMANCE OF HIS JOB DUTIES AS A PATIENT CARE ASSISTANT WHEN HE RETIRED 1 1/2 YEARS EARLIER ON SEPTEMBER 1, 2010. FURTHER, PETITIONER HAS NOT SHOWN THAT, EVEN IF DR. ROSENBAUM'S REPORT WERE RELEVANT, THAT IN THE EXERCISE OF REASONABLE DILIGENCE IT COULD NOT HAVE BEEN PRODUCED IN CONNECTION WITH PETITIONER'S APPLICATION FOR ORDINARY DISABILITY RETIREMENT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.
ORDER DENYING MOTION TO AUGMENT RECORD
AMENDED PROOF OF SERVICE OF NOTICE OF MOTION AND POINTS AND AUTHORITIES AND DECLARATION FILED BY PETITIONER KING, FRANK L.

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Mandatory Dismissal
Off Calendar Apr-18-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Rebecca Liu, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, T:415.554.3870, for defendant/moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling.

RESPONDENT SAN FRANCISCO EMPLOYEES' RETIREMENT SYSTEM's Motion For Mandatory Dismissal is granted. No opposition filed.

ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Not reported.

Notice Of Motion To Augment Record
Off Calendar Apr-16-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: ALBERT MIKLOS KUN, ESQ., 415.362.4000, FOR PETITIONER FRANK KING.
REBECCA LIU, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, 415.554.3800, FOR RESPONDENT SAN FRANCISCO EMPLOYEES' RETIREMENT SYSTEM.

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: PETITIONER FRANK KING Motion To Augment Record is denied. As to the September 16, 2012 Social Security Administration decision, Petitioner failed to make a threshold showing that the Social Security Administration decision is relevant for the purposes of analyzing Petitioner's application for ordinary disability retirement, which is based on a different legal standard. (See Code of Civil Procedure 1094.5(e).) As to Dr. Rosenbaum's medical report, Petitioner has failed to show that it is relevant. Dr. Rosenbaum's report was based on an exam that took place in April 2012. Petitioner has not shown how these findings are relevant to the issue whether Petitioner was incapacitated for the performance of his job duties as a patient care assistant when he retired 1 and 1/2 years earlier on September 1, 2010. Further, Petitioner has not shown that, even if Dr. Rosenbaum's report were relevant, that in the exercise of reasonable diligence it could not have been produced in connection with Petitioner's application for ordinary disability retirement. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

Notice Of Motion To Augment Record
Off Calendar Mar-25-2013 Continued To Apr-16-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONER FRANK KING's Motion To Augment Record is Continued to April 16, 2013 on the Court's own motion. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
